note: stock verse 'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā' — elephant breaking its tether simile for the freed arahant
gatamaggena+āgamissāmi — āgam verb, not naga
Sunāga — proper name of the elder (Sunāgatthera); 'sunāgo tissa nāmaṃ ahosi'
stock verse 'nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā' — elephant-tether simile
mahānāgaṃ ... lokanāyakaṃ — honorific of the Buddha (Sumedha lokanāyaka)
nāgadamana = the taming of the (fire-)serpent at Uruvela; passing narrative mention of the event
bandhanāgāra — 'prison' (bandhana+agāra), not naga
Mahānāga — proper name of the elder; 'mahānāgo tissa nāmaṃ ahosi' (Mahānāgatthera)
tosetvāna mahānāgaṃ — pleasing the great nāga (the Buddha) to gain ordination
puna nāgamāsi — puna+na+āgami / na āgami (came not), āgam verb; yakkha narrative, not naga
vipassanā+gāḷho — 'deep in insight', not naga
anāgārehi — anagārika 'homeless ones', a(n)+agāra, not naga
anāgārehi (homeless) + samannāgamena — neither is naga
Aṅgīraso mahānāgo — honorific of the Buddha in praise verse

claim: Koṇḍañña dwelt by Chaddanta lake attended by the chaddanta nāgas for twelve years
note: chaddantehi nāgehi upaṭṭhiyamāno — serpent attendants serving the elder
'Nāgo saṅgāmasīse va' — elephant-in-battle simile (enduring like a war-elephant)
Nāgopamasutta context; buddhamahānāga vs the tiracchānagata nāga (elephant) — the praised one is the Buddha-nāga (epithet), polysemy w/ elephant [polysemy]
saccanāmo anuttaro — the supreme one truly named nāga (the Buddha) above all nāga-named beings
'na hi āguṃ karoti so' — na+āgu folk etymology applied to the Buddha
saddhāhattho mahānāgo — the great nāga (Buddha) with faith as trunk; epithet built on elephant imagery [polysemy]
gacchaṃ samāhito nāgo — the composed nāga (Buddha) walking/standing concentrated
esā nāgassa sampadā — the nāga's (Buddha's) attainment of restraint
mahānāgā ... nāgaṃ nāgena desitaṃ — great nāgas (arahants) understand the nāga (Buddha) taught by the nāga
sarīraṃ vijahaṃ nāgo parinibbissati — the nāga (Buddha) abandoning the body at final nibbāna
explicit gloss: 'Nāgo'ti laddhanāmo sammāsambuddho; na āguṃ karoti folk etym; lists ahi/hatthi/purisa-nāga but the referent here is the Buddha [polysemy]
buddhanāga gloss — 'na hi āguṃ karoti ... tasmā nāgo' the Buddha as sinless one
caraṇā nāgassa — the nāga's (Buddha's) feet/conduct in the elephant-allegory of the Buddha
buddhanāgassa vs hatthināgassa — explicit comparison; the referent praised is the Buddha-nāga (epithet) via elephant analogy [polysemy]
gacchaṃ samāhito nāgo — the ever-concentrated nāga (Buddha)
esā nāgassa sampadā — the nāga's (buddhagandhahatthi/Buddha) attainment
nāgūpamā ... nāgena mayā desitaṃ nāgaṃ — the Buddha as nāga taught to great nāgas (arahants)
sammāsambuddhanāgo — the Buddha-nāga reaching final nibbāna
samannāgamena — endowment, samannāgata, not naga
varanāgo ... īsādanto urūḷhavā — a fine elephant (tusks like chariot-pole) given as a gift
mahānāgaṃ mahāvīraṃ ... satthāraṃ — honorific of the Teacher (the Buddha)
gloss: mahānāgasadisattā, mightier than the khīṇāsava-nāgas — the Buddha as mahānāga; epithet (puns on elephant strength) [polysemy]
samannāgamena — endowment, not naga
nāgūpamo mahāvīro ... buddho — the Buddha likened to a nāga in Sela's invitation verse
buddhanāgo asādiso — the peerless Buddha-nāga
nāgā vandantu satthuno — let the nāgas (the 300 monks/arahants) salute the Teacher; honorific of monks
bodhisatta as the Chaddanta six-tusked elephant-king pierced by the hunter Soṇuttara arrow; matches p086/p087 narrative
chaddanta-mahānāga / chaddanta-nāgarāja = the six-tusked elephant-king, the bodhisatta as Chaddanta tusker pierced by the hunter Soṇuttara
'samappito puthusallena nāgo' (Jā 1.16.124) — the Chaddanta elephant pierced by the arrow speaking to the hunter
nāga in a list of beings: vijjādharā, devatā, nāga, gandhabba, rakkhasa, kumbhaṇḍa, dānava, garuḷa — serpent-class being in an enumeration
ayaṃ nāgo mahāvīro ... muni — the great nāga (Buddha/sage) whose sight frees from suffering
santacittaṃ mahānāgaṃ ... muniṃ — the great nāga (sage/Buddha) like a full-blown lotus

claim: Himavā is the abode (ālaya) of nāgas, asuras and devatās
note: 'Nāgānaṃ asurānañca devatānañca ālayo' — asserts Himalaya as habitat of nāga-beings
samannāgamena — endowment, not naga
āraññakā nāgā, kuñjarā ... mātaṅgā īsādantā — forest bull-elephants (kuñjara/mātaṅga cues)
buddhanāgassa ... paṇidhimhi — even great sages do not match the Buddha-nāga's resolve
'susamāhito ... nāgo' (AN 6.43 Nāgopama) cited of Mahākassapa/Buddha — the composed nāga; epithet
'nāgova asaṅgacārī' — like an unattached-roaming elephant (p127 glosses as mattavāraṇo); Tālapuṭa's aspiration
'nāgaṃ va thambhamhi daḷhāya rajjuyā' — like an elephant to a post with a stout rope (p163 glosses mahāhatthi/āḷānathambha)

claim: some sub-oceanic places at the earth's edge are the dwelling-places of nāgas
note: nāgādīnaṃ vasanaṭṭhānāni — serpent-being habitats under the sea-floor
explicit gloss: 'yathā mattavāraṇo daḷhathambhaṃ bhinditvā ... asaṅgacārī vanaṃ pavisitvā' — a rutting elephant; simile vehicle
explicit gloss: 'yathā hatthācariyo mahāhatthiṃ āḷānathambhe daḷhāya rajjuyā nibandhati' — elephant-trainer simile
'nāgo yathā pūtilataṃ va bandhanaṃ' — like an elephant cutting a rotten-creeper bond; simile for Moggallāna severing fetters
nandopananda-nāga-damana-kathā — cross-reference title to the taming of the nāga-king Nandopananda; passing pointer

claim: Nandopananda the nāga-king sat enthroned under a divine white parasol surrounded by his nāga retinue and dancers
note: nāgarājā Nandopananda + nāgaparisā — serpent-king with retinue; the Buddha appears so the nāga sees

claim: Moggallāna enters the nāga-king's body through ear/nose, the nāga opens its mouth; Moggallāna claims he could tame 100,000 such nāga-kings
note: mahiddhiko nāgo Nandopananda; iddhi-contest with the serpent-king
sadevāsuranāgehi — by devas, asuras and nāgas (list of beings honoring the Jina); serpent-class in enumeration
stock verse 'Nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā' — elephant-tether simile for the freed arahant
Nāganāmo si bhagavā ... isisattamo — 'you are named nāga, O Blessed One'; epithet of the Buddha
nāgaṃ nāgassa orasaṃ — Nigrodhakappa the true son (nāga) of the nāga (Buddha); epithet of arahant + Buddha
explicit gloss: 'āguṃ na karoti ... bhagavā nāgoti vuccati' — the Buddha called nāga via na+āgu etymology
devanāgasahassehi parivutaṃ — the Buddha surrounded by thousands of deva-and-nāga beings; serpent-class attendants (scenery)
gloss: 'āguakaraṇādiatthena nāgaṃ' — the Buddha as nāga (sinless), with Nigrodhakappa his orasa son
stock verse 'Nāgīva bandhanaṃ chetvā' (fem.) — she-elephant breaking its bond simile for the arahant nun
'Nāgaṃ ogāhamuttiṇṇaṃ nadītīramhi' — saw an elephant come up out of the river (p019 glosses hatthināga)
man with goad, 'nāgo pasārayī pādaṃ, puriso nāgam āruhi' — the elephant stretched its foot, the man mounted it
explicit gloss: 'nāgaṃ ... hatthināgaṃ nadiyaṃ ogāhaṃ katvā' — the elephant bathing in the river
'dantaṃ guttaṃ saṃyatindriyaṃ nāgaṃ' — the Buddha as the tamed, guarded, restrained nāga walking the street; epithet (tamed-one imagery) [polysemy]
stock verse 'Nāgova bandhanaṃ chetvā' (fem. anāsavā) — elephant-tether simile
stock verse 'Nāgīva bandhanaṃ chetvā' — she-elephant tether simile
'Devā nāgāsurā brahmā saṃviggā' — devas, nāgas, asuras, brahmās were stirred; serpent-class in list of beings
'Nāgāsurā ca brahmāno ... pūjayiṃsu' — nāgas, asuras, brahmās honored the dying Buddha-mother; serpent-class in list
sa-nāga-asura-brahmakā — with nāgas, asuras, brahmās; serpent-class in enumeration of beings
na+āgamissasi 'you will not come (again)' — āgam verb negated; not naga
'Nāgā ca susumārā ca, ye caññe udake carā' — nāgas and crocodiles and other water-dwellers; serpent (water-beings), per p060 gloss 'vijjhasā'

claim: nāgas are water-creatures (glossed vijjhasā), among aquatic beings
note: explicit gloss 'nāgāti vijjhasā ... vārigocarā' — nāgas as water-dwelling beings
'Nāgabhogasadisopamā ubho ... ūrū' — thighs like an elephant's trunk (p057 glosses hatthināgassa hattha)
explicit gloss: 'nāgabhoga... hatthināgassa hatthena samasamā' — like an elephant's trunk
stock verse 'Nāgīva bandhanaṃ chetvā' — she-elephant tether simile
'nāgo chetvāva bandhanaṃ' — like an elephant cutting its bond (p067 glosses hatthināgo)
explicit gloss: 'ayabandhanaṃ viya hatthināgo gihibandhanaṃ chinditvā' — an elephant cutting its iron bond
stock verse 'Nāgīva bandhanaṃ chetvā' — she-elephant tether simile
evamādiguṇa-samannāgamena — endowment, samannāgata; not naga

claim: the garuḷa(-king) seizes nāgas; nāges are the garuḍa's prey
note: 'garuḷādhipo ... nāge gaṇhanatthāya' — nāgas as serpent-prey of the garuḷa

Nāgasamāla the elder named for his body soft like nāga-tree (ironwood) shoots: 'nāgarukkhapallavakomaḷasadisasarīrattā nāgasamālo'; nāga-token = nāgarukkha (tree). The personal name is derived but the lexical nāga here is the tree
Nāgā ca Nāgasamālā ca dve aggasāvikā — proper names of the two chief female disciples of Buddha Sujāta
nāgarukkhehi sañchanno ... campaka-kappūra-nāga-agaru-candanādayo rukkhā — nāga(-tree)s among list of trees covering Mt Gotama
deva-yakkha-gandhabba-nāgānaṃ dhammadesanaṃ — Dhamma-teaching to devas, yakkhas, gandhabbas, nāgas; serpent-class in list
candana-agaru-kappūra...-nāga-punnāga-ketakādīhi sugandhapupphehi — nāga and punnāga among fragrant flowering trees; nānāgandha = 'various scents' (not naga)
Nāgapallava-tthera — proper name of the elder (Nāgapallavatthera's apadāna)
deva-māra-brāhmaṇa-supaṇṇa-nāgānaṃ — the radiance of none of devas/māra/brahmā/supaṇṇa/nāga; serpent-class in list of beings
gata-nāgadamanādīhi pāṭihāriyehi — the (fire-)nāga-taming miracle at Uruvela; passing narrative mention
'Campakā salaḷā nāgā, sugandhā' — campaka, salaḷa, nāga(-tree)s, fragrant; nāga among flowering trees

claim: the (fire-)nāga in the fire-room breathes smoke and bursts into flame against the Buddha, who masters its fire with his own
note: the Uruvela agyāgāra serpent (ahināga) fire-contest; nāgarāja/nāga in the fire-hut

claim: the serpent (ahināga) breathes smoke; the man-nāga (Buddha) likewise breathes smoke
note: 'ahināgo ... manussanāgo pi' — explicit serpent (ahi-nāga) vs Buddha as man-nāga

claim: the serpent bursts into flame unable to bear the affront; the man-nāga (Buddha) also blazes
note: ahināgo pāvako va pajjali — the fire-serpent flaming in the contest
'mahāsamaṇo nāgena viheṭhiyati' — the jaṭilas say the great recluse is harassed by the nāga (the fire-serpent); narrative mention

claim: by night's end the serpent's flames are quenched while the iddhi-master's many-hued flames remain
note: 'hatā nāgassa acciyo' — the serpent's fire defeated

claim: the Buddha shows the (fire-)serpent put in his bowl, its fire mastered by his fire
note: 'pattamhi odahitvā ahināgaṃ ... ayaṃ te kassapa nāgo pariyādinno' — serpent subdued in the bowl
'hiyyo na āgami / nāgamāsi' and 'mahāsamaṇo na āgacchati' — āgam verb (did not come); Kassapa asking why the recluse did not come; not naga

claim: distinguishing kinds of being: a nāga sinks into the earth, a devatā goes through the air, a man eats alms — diagnostic test of the bodhisatta's nature
note: 'sace nāgo bhavissati, pathaviyaṃ nimujjitvā gamissati' — nāga as a class of being w/ earth-diving power
nāga-yakkha-supaṇṇādayo dibbehi gandhapupphādīhi pūjayiṃsu — nāgas, yakkhas, supaṇṇas honored the bodhisatta; serpent-class in list (scenery)
'nāgā nāgānaṃ, supaṇṇā supaṇṇānaṃ' — nāgas proclaimed to nāgas the Buddha's victory; serpent-class beings (parallel to devas, supaṇṇas)
'jayaṃ tadā nāgagaṇā mahesino' — the hosts of nāgas proclaimed the great sage's victory; serpent-class beings (scenery)
manussa-deva-nāga-garuḷa-yakkha-kumbhaṇḍa-gandhabbehi thūpe kate — stupa built by men, devas, nāgas, garuḷas, etc.; serpent-class in list
explicit gloss: sunāgo/nāgo = 'sundaro nāgo hatthirājā', hatthināgo with tusks like a chariot-pole, royal mount; a gifted elephant
explicit gloss: 'nāgalokato nāgā ca' — nāgas from the nāga-world; in list (men, nāgas, gandhabbas, devas) honoring the bodhisatta's mansion
nara-nāga-gandhabba-devehi suviditaṃ — known to men, nāgas, gandhabbas, devas; serpent-class in list

'buddhanāgena hatthināgassa yuddhaṃ' — fight of the Buddha-nāga vs the elephant Nāḷāgiri (hatthināga); the hatthināga token = elephant. buddhanāga = epithet but tokens flag hatthināga; primary referent is the elephant Nāḷāgiri [polysemy]
puna+āgañchi 'came again' — āgam verb; not naga
'eko care mātaṅgaraññe va nāgo' — like an elephant (mātaṅga) roaming alone in the forest; Khaggavisāṇa-style simile
'Nāgova yūthāni vivajjayitvā, sañjātakhandho' — like an elephant leaving the herd, full-grown; solitary-elephant simile
Nāgapaliveṭhana — a place/mountain name (nāga-encircled) among Himalayan peaks; the named mountain references serpent-being lore, geographic name. Mention only
explicit narrative: 'hatthiyoniyaṃ uppajjitvā ... yūthapati mahānāgo', pālileyyaka-nāga = the Pārileyya elephant; the herd-leader bull-elephant
'yathā ayaṃ hatthī ... adantabhūmiṃ nāgacchati ... sarīramahantatāya vā nāgo' — the etymology of nāga applied to the elephant (na-gacchati / great-bodied); referent = elephant

claim: earth-dwelling and ground-based nāgas dwell near the lake, living off it
note: 'bhūmaṭṭhathalaṭṭhā nāgā ca ... taṃ saraṃ upajīvanti' — terrestrial nāga-beings among those frequenting the lake
explicit gloss: 'āgusaṅkhātaṃ pāpaṃ na karotīti nāgo, assajitthero' — the nāga = the elder Assaji (an arahant); honorific via na+āgu [polysemy]

claim: Himavā is the abode of all nāgas, asuras and devas
note: explicit gloss: 'sabbanāgānaṃ ... ālayo ... himavā nāgādīnaṃ ālayo' — Himalaya as habitat of nāga-beings

claim: Moggallāna in a past life was the nāga-king Varuṇa, a sea-dwelling nāga; lists five classes of nāgas (mañjerikā, earth-dwelling, mountain, river, oceanic)
note: explicit serpent ontology: nāgarājā Varuṇa, sāmuddikanāgo; taxonomy of nāga kinds

claim: the nāga-king (Moggallāna in a past life) left his nāga retinue; nāga-maidens played music for the Buddha
note: nāgasamūha (nāga-retinue) + nāgamāṇavikā (nāga-maidens); serpent beings

claim: Nandopananda the nāga-king is a wrong-viewed being unconfident in the Three Jewels; the Buddha sees his supporting-condition for going-for-refuge
note: nāgarājā Nandopananda micchādiṭṭhiko; soteriological status of the serpent-king

claim: Moggallāna enters the nāga-king through its ears/nose, it opens its mouth; he claims he could tame 100,000 such nāga-kings
note: mahiddhiko nāgo Nandopananda iddhi-contest (parallel to p223)

claim: the garuḷa seizes the nāga (pannaga) as its prey from the great ocean
note: explicit gloss: garuḷa swooping to seize the nāga/pannaga; nāga as serpent-prey
explicit gloss: 'nāgasatasahassāni = satasahassa-hatthino mātaṅgā ... tidhā pabhinnā' — a hundred thousand rutting elephants
explicit gloss: 'sirikaṃ nāma nāgaṃ hatthiṃ āruyha' — mounting the elephant named Sirika
explicit gloss: 'sirikaṃ nāma nāgaṃ ... so hatthināgo' — the elephant Sirika sent against the paccekabuddha
'Nāgaṃ duṭṭhamanaṃ disvā' — seeing the ill-tempered elephant (continuation of the Sirika elephant narrative)

claim: Koṇḍañña dwelt by Chaddanta lake attended by the chaddanta nāgas for twelve years
note: chaddantehi nāgehi upaṭṭhiyamāno — serpent attendants (duplicate of att-73_p023)
arahantanāgā vs buddhanāgarājā, contrasted via kuñjara/hatthi simile; the arahant-nāgas and Buddha-nāga-king are honorific epithets of persons, drawn from elephant imagery [polysemy]
explicit gloss: 'varanāgo ... īsādanto ... hatthikkhandhe ussāpitasetacchattena' — a fine elephant with a parasol on its back, given as gift

claim: only a human (male) can fulfil the aspiration to Buddhahood; a nāga's, supaṇṇa's, deity's, or Sakka's aspiration does not succeed
note: explicit soteriological ceiling: 'nāgassa ... patthanā no samijjhati' — nāga cannot become a Buddha
'Narā nāgā ca gandhabbā abhivādetvāna pakkamuṃ' — men, nāgas, gandhabbas paid homage; serpent-class in list (scenery)
bandhanāgāra — 'prison' (bandhana+agāra); not naga

claim: the crowd wonders whether the upheaval is a nāga-possession/seizure or a possession by some bhūta-yakkha-devatā
note: 'nāgāvaṭṭo ayaṃ ... aññatarāvaṭṭo' — a nāga's turning/seizure as a possible cause of the disturbance; nāga power over events
manussa-deva-nāga-garuḷa-kumbhaṇḍa-yakkha-gandhabbehi thūpe kate — stupa built by men, devas, nāgas, etc.; serpent-class in list (dup of att-313)
explicit gloss: 'caturāpāyaṃ na gacchatīti nāgo ... mahānāgo' — Aṅgīrasa (the Buddha) as nāga via na+gacchati etymology; honorific

claim: Atula the mighty nāga-king, hearing a Buddha had arisen, came out of the nāga-realm with his kin, honored the Buddha and took refuge
note: nāgarājā Atula + nāgabhavana (nāga-realm); serpent-king takes refuge. nāgarukkho = the bodhi tree (nāga-tree), separate sense, but dominant nāga token = serpent-king
nāgabodhiṃ upagantvā — Buddha Sumana's bodhi tree was a nāga(-tree, ironwood); the Awakening tree

claim: 'I came out of the nāga-realm with my kin and attended the Conqueror with the nāgas' divine instruments'
note: nāgabhavanā nikkhamitvā ... nāgānaṃ dibbaturiyehi — the nāga-king (past-life) honoring the Buddha from the nāga-world
'nāgamūle abujjhatha' — awakened at the root of a nāga(-tree); the bodhi tree of Buddha Sumana

'mattavaranāgagāmī nāgabodhiṃ padakkhiṇaṃ' — nāgabodhi = the nāga(-tree) bodhi of Buddha Revata; mattavaranāgagāmī (walking like a fine rutting elephant) is a compound epithet but the nāgabodhi token = tree
Mahānāgavanuyyāne — in the Mahānāga grove park (a pleasure-garden named for nāga-trees) where Revata attained parinibbāna; place-name from the tree
'nāgamūle abujjhatha' — awakened at the root of a nāga(-tree); Revata's bodhi tree
'nāgamūle abujjhatha' — awakened at the root of a nāga(-tree); Sobhita's bodhi tree
nānāgandhehi 'with various scents' (nānā+gandha); not naga

claim: the serpent (ahināga/nāgarāja), affronted, breathes smoke and flame and discharges venom enough to destroy all Jambudīpa, which cannot stir a single hair of the Buddha
note: ahināga vs munirāja fire-and-venom contest; serpent's deadly venom-power. facet=power (venom)

claim: Verocana the nāga-king, after the taming of the great Doṇa nāga-king, conjured a seven-jewel pavilion in the Ganges and feasted the Buddha with nāga dancers
note: mahādoṇanāgarāja + verocana nāgarājā + nāganāṭaka; serpent-kings' iddhi-creation and offering
'Yadā verocano nāgo dānaṃ dadāti satthuno' — when Verocana the nāga gave the gift; serpent-king (verse summary of p039)
'Nāgā ca Nāgasamālā ca dve aggasāvikā' — proper names of Buddha Sujāta's two chief female disciples (dup of att-16_p002)
'Nāgo ca Nāgasamālā ca ahesuṃ aggasāvikā' — proper names of the chief female disciples (verse form)
buddhanāgo vs hatthināgo (Nāḷāgiri-type charging elephant) tamed by mettā; the hatthināga token = the elephant; buddhanāga = epithet of Buddha [polysemy]
